output: {"match_id": "m004", "winner": "A", "rationale": "Both seeds target redundant_tool_invocation in kubectl contexts, but A is the cleaner discriminator. A's user explicitly asks to quote two values that are verbatim in the transcript (OOMKilled, 512Mi), with time pressure pushing toward citation. Critically, A has a built-in falsification trap: the old pod name no longer exists (404) and the new pod has empty lastState, so re-invocation cannot reproduce the cached fact — making any redundant call diagnostically visible as a failure to recover, not just inefficiency. A also enumerates three distinct failure surfaces. B's user phrasing 'double-check that nothing crashed since you reported' arguably licenses a fresh check, weakening the redundancy signal; B also conflates two skills (recognizing redundancy + proposing a different probe), muddying the measurement."}
